methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Fourth Power is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Asegun Henry with $150M post-money valuation and $39M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of eight private companies in the same sector and stage, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA). Notable peers include Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B Series B), Pano AI ($500M), and Sage Geosystems ($400M), alongside companies like Reverion and CarbonCapture Inc. trading in the $300M range.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical from Series A to subsequent stages, projects $300M from the current $150M base. Peer-median analysis across the eight-company cohort yields $305M, with the broader range spanning $110M to $5.0B. The stage-progression and peer-median methods converge closely, both supporting valuations near $300M.

The triangulated range spans $255M (low) to $351M (high), with a median of $302M. This suggests the current $150M valuation sits materially below peer benchmarks and typical stage-step progression. Key uncertainties include execution risk on commercialization timelines, the wide dispersion within the peer set (driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion's outlier valuation), and early-stage burn dynamics relative to capital raised.
